title: Reroute RAT-362 to control-plane repo owning /api/issues lifecycle
description:
Scope: move/attach execution of RAT-362 to the repository/workspace that owns Paperclip control-plane issue lifecycle runtime (transition/checkout/wake dedupe) so done->in_progress auto-reopen suppression can be implemented and validated.

Deliverables:
1) RAT-362 execution attached to correct repo/workspace.
2) Implementation task references exact runtime files/routes for done-state reopen guard.
3) Verification evidence from issue status history attached.

Unblock target: parent RAT-362.

title: RAT-22D Corregir reapertura automatica de RAT-22 (status loop)
description:
RAT-22 se reabre repetidamente a `in_progress` sin comentarios nuevos ni cambios de alcance, generando wakes redundantes.

Objetivo:
- Identificar causa raiz del trigger de reapertura.
- Corregir regla/workflow para evitar status loop en issues cerrados.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Causa raiz documentada.
2. Fix aplicado en automatizacion/regla.
3. Validacion: RAT-22 no vuelve a reabrirse sin señal funcional real.

title: Stale sweep wave 1: CTO owns lifecycle/status-drift cluster closure
description: Triage and close or consolidate stale todo/in_progress items in lifecycle status-drift cluster. Route true platform fixes to active tracks and close duplicate stale tickets with references.

title: Implement terminal done guard to prevent no-delta auto-reopen (RAT-188 class)
description:
Scope: patch Paperclip control-plane lifecycle runtime so issue status cannot drift from done to todo/in_progress without explicit resume intent or new material scope.

Required changes:
1) Add terminal-state guard in /api/issues transition/wake/sweep paths.
2) Require explicit resume signal (e.g., structured resume=true or explicit human reopen) before leaving done.
3) Add idempotent dedupe for no-delta status-change events.
4) Add regression tests covering RAT-188 timeline equivalents.

Acceptance:
- RAT-188 remains done across heartbeats unless explicit human reopen with new scope.
- Regression tests fail before patch and pass after patch.
- Issue thread includes test evidence and touched files.

title: Investigate repeated status drift reopening closed issue RAT-188
description:
Parent context: [RAT-188](/RAT/issues/RAT-188).

Observed behavior:
- RAT-188 has been set to `done` multiple times with acceptance evidence satisfied, but status repeatedly drifts back to `in_progress` without new contradictory technical evidence.

Required action:
- Identify automation/policy/process that reopens closed issues in this path.
- Provide fix or guardrail so terminal `done` state persists when no new work is introduced.
- Document root cause and permanent remediation in issue thread.

Acceptance:
- RAT-188 remains `done` across subsequent heartbeats unless explicit human reopen with new scope.

title: Sweep stale in_progress issues (no active run) and force state correction
description:
Context: RAT-555 found a large stale queue in status=in_progress with activeRun=false.

Scope:
1) Review all in_progress issues with activeRun=false and lastActivity older than 2 hours.
2) For each issue, enforce one of:
- resume execution now with concrete next action comment, or
- move to todo with explicit handoff note, or
- move to blocked with blockedByIssueIds + unblock owner/action.
3) Close stale loops where review tasks are silently parked.

Done criteria:
- No stale in_progress issue remains without either active execution or explicit state correction.
- Thread comments include unblock owner/action for every blocked transition.

title: Stale sweep: collapse status-flapping/reopen bug cluster into single remediation track
description: From RAT-586 triage: multiple stale todo/in_progress issues are variants of the same done->in_progress reopen defect. Consolidate into one fix plan, close duplicates, and leave migration notes for affected issues.

title: Investigate and stop repeated auto-reopen of completed review issues
description:
Root-cause and remediate repeated status drift where completed review issues (example: RAT-109) are automatically set back to in_progress without new actionable deltas.

Scope:
- Identify automation/routine/rule that mutates done->in_progress.
- Add guard so closed review issues only reopen when new actionable context exists.
- Provide verification evidence with before/after issue lifecycle traces.

Acceptance:
- RCA documented.
- Fix deployed/configured.
- RAT-109 remains done across subsequent status-change cycles.

title: Stability: RAT-46 false reopen loop on issue_blockers_resolved
description:
Investigate and fix repeated RAT-46 lifecycle drift where wake payload reports in_progress/todo despite issue being closed as done with no new comments.

Expected behavior:
- Closed issue should not be reopened by blocker-resolution wake when there is no new actionable delta.
- Continuation summary acceptance criteria should stay in sync with issue description.

Deliverables:
1. Root cause analysis with reproduction steps.
2. Fix in wake/state transition path.
3. Regression test covering closed-issue wake reconciliation.
4. Backfill note in runbook/changelog.

title: Implement no-delta terminal reopen guard for RAT-233 drift path in issue lifecycle runtime
description:
Context: RAT-233 (done) repeatedly flips to in_progress on issue_status_changed wakes with no new comments/scope delta. Investigation from RAT-408 confirms this is control-plane lifecycle behavior, not uber-services domain code.

Scope:
- Trace the precise transition path that mutates done->in_progress for RAT-233-equivalent issues (continuation/review wake, checkout, dependency/status hooks).
- Enforce terminal guardrail: done/cancelled cannot reopen without explicit resume:true intent + auditable reason.
- Suppress no-delta status-change wake mutations that only churn lifecycle state.
- Add regression tests for: (1) terminal + automation/no resume => no reopen, (2) explicit resume:true => reopen allowed.
- Attach replay evidence using RAT-233-equivalent fixture and timestamps around 2026-05-11T03:32Z-03:58Z.

Acceptance:
- RAT-233 remains done across repeated issue_status_changed wakes unless explicit resume:true mutation is submitted.
- Evidence (test names + replay log) posted in issue comments.

title: Investigate RAT-133 status auto-reopen churn
description:
Repeated lifecycle drift observed: [RAT-133](/RAT/issues/RAT-133) reverts to `in_progress` after being set `done` without new scope/comments.

Objective:
- identify automation or policy path reopening completed issues
- implement guard so closed issues are not reactivated without concrete delta
- document fix and verification evidence

Expected output:
- root cause
- remediation change
- proof that repeated reopen loop is stopped

title: Attach control-plane workspace ownership for issue-lifecycle fix lane
description:
Status: unblock artifact for [RAT-393](/RAT/issues/RAT-393) and [RAT-362](/RAT/issues/RAT-362).

- Problem: execution workspace only includes uber-services; control-plane repo owning `/api/issues` lifecycle runtime is not attached.
- Required outcome: attach/transfer to control-plane repo/workspace and assign runtime owner to execute lifecycle guard implementation.

Owner/action:
- Owner: @board
- Action: provision/attach correct repo workspace and route assignee.

title: RAT-386 follow-up: prevent repeated auto-reopen after review closure
description:
Problem
RAT-386 was repeatedly moved from done back to in_progress without new evidence, creating review-lane churn and duplicate wakeups.

Required outcome
1. Keep productivity-review issues terminal after closure unless explicit resume:true or substantive new evidence is attached.
2. Suppress/no-op status flips driven only by watchdog refresh comments.
3. Add regression tests for repeated reopen loops on completed review issues.

Acceptance
- Repro case for RAT-386 style loop is covered by test.
- No automatic transition done->in_progress without explicit resume path.
- Wake noise for this class is reduced to zero in dry-run replay.

title: CTO sweep: clear blocked needs_attention cluster (29 issues)
description:
Scope: all CTO-owned issues currently `blocked` with `blockerAttention=needs_attention` in the RAT-706 board snapshot (2026-05-11).

Required actions per issue:
1) Convert blocker into first-class `blockedByIssueIds` OR move issue to `todo/in_progress` if no real upstream blocker exists.
2) Add dated next action + unblock owner in comments.
3) If ownership is wrong, reassign to the execution profile that can close it this sprint.

Done when: every issue in this cluster has either an explicit blocker link with owner/action or is moved to actionable status with an active owner.

title: RAT-591 sweep shard: consolidate 2 duplicates owned by 516aa272 onto RAT-568
description:
Execute stale-sweep consolidation for your owned duplicate cluster under [RAT-591](/RAT/issues/RAT-591).

1. For each issue below, set either `done` (merged into RAT-568) or `blocked` with `blockedByIssueIds=[RAT-568 id]` when ownership/history must persist.

3. Post per-issue ledger (issue -> action -> rationale) as a comment on RAT-591.

Duplicates in scope:
- [RAT-449](/RAT/issues/RAT-449)
- [RAT-451](/RAT/issues/RAT-451)

Acceptance:
- No listed duplicate remains `in_progress` without unique vector evidence.

- [RAT-24] QA reproducibility review -> QA Specialist

Acceptance criteria:

title: Harden RAT-41 sweep: do not auto-unblock RAT-290/RAT-191 without runtime guard READY evidence
description:

title: RAT-5 Iteración 1: Review QA del spec de rating 360
description:
Revisar el documento spec de [RAT-5](/RAT/issues/RAT-5#document-spec) y ejecutar iteración 1 de calidad/QA.

title: Control-plane fix: expose writable blocker-link mutation contract for blocked issues
description:
Parent: RAT-580.

Need control-plane API support for blocker normalization:
1) writable blocker-link field/endpoint that persists canonical blocker ids and is returned by GET issue payload,
2) privileged/authorized cross-agent normalization path for CTO sweeps,
3) replay evidence showing mutations persist and cycle/ACL behavior are explicit.

Acceptance:
- CTO can set blocker links as first-class issue dependencies for blocked issues lacking links.
- Contract documented in thread with example request/response.

title: Fix issue status governor: prevent done->in_progress auto-flip without new scoped input
description:
Status-governor defect follow-up from [RAT-178](/RAT/issues/RAT-178).

Context:
- Completed issues are being auto-overridden from `done` to `in_progress` immediately after closure, even with no new scope/comments.
- This breaks completion semantics and creates false re-open signals.

Required outcome:
- Preserve `done` unless a valid reopen trigger exists (new scoped comment, explicit status change, or execution-policy event).
- Add regression coverage around close/reopen transitions.
- Document reopen trigger rules in workflow automation docs.

title: Implement admin bulk stale in_progress correction endpoint (conflict-safe)
description: RAT-556 exposed that per-issue PATCH corrections hit 409 lock conflicts and cannot complete full stale sweep. Implement a control-plane admin endpoint/job that atomically transitions stale in_progress issues (activeRunId=null, executionRunId=null) to todo with audit metadata, without requiring per-issue checkout ownership. Include dry-run mode and summary counts.

title: Staging/Production actor-signing rollout evidence for RAT-134
description:
RAT-134 security closure requires non-local enforcement evidence; RAT-218 delivered tooling and local proof only.

Required deliverables:
1) Staging proof: ACTOR_SIGNING_SECRET provisioned
2) Production proof: ACTOR_SIGNING_SECRET provisioned
3) Staging proof: ACTOR_SIGNING_ENFORCED=true active
4) Production proof: ACTOR_SIGNING_ENFORCED=true active
5) Staging smoke artifact via tools/guardrails/generate-rat-218-evidence.sh
6) Production smoke artifact via tools/guardrails/generate-rat-218-evidence.sh
7) 24h staging/prod AUTHENTICATION_ERROR summaries via tools/guardrails/actor-signing-monitoring-summary.js

References:
- docs/trust-safety/rat-134-actor-signing-contract.md
- qa/test-results/rat-218-closure-evidence-bundle-2026-05-11.md (section 4 pending list)

title: Dedupe de auto-wake issue_status_changed para cierres terminales
description:
Corregir la automatizacion de wake para evitar re-disparos que reabren flujo en cierres terminales sin delta real.

Alcance:
- Suprimir wake `issue_status_changed` cuando el cambio sea solo finalizacion terminal y no haya delta de comentario, blockers o assignee.
- Verificar que checkout sobre issue terminal no muta estado sin resume explicito.
- Mantener wakes vigentes para cambios no terminales o con delta real.

Criterios de aceptacion:
1. Transiciones de cierre terminal sin delta no generan wake redundante.
2. Replays equivalentes a RAT-35/RAT-52 no producen reopen automatico.
3. Evidencia de run/eventos adjunta en thread.

title: RAT-26 Impl: migraciones y lifecycle backend reviews v1
description:
Objetivo: ejecutar implementación backend base del dominio de reviews según contrato v1 validado en RAT-26.

Alcance:
- Crear migraciones SQL para `reviews`, `review_tags`, `review_events`, `review_reports`, `review_responses`, `review_appeals`, `review_aggregates`.
- Implementar constraints de integridad e idempotencia (`unique (ride_id, author_user_id, author_role)` + manejo `Idempotency-Key`).
- Implementar transiciones válidas de lifecycle y rechazo con `409 INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION`.
- Agregar tests de integración para happy path + edge cases de concurrencia.

Fuera de alcance:
- UI y copy.
- Algoritmo avanzado not-recommended.

Criterios de aceptación:
- Migraciones aplican y revierten sin pérdida.
- Tests cubren create/edit/respond/appeal/report con casos de carrera.
- Documentación de errores estándar incluida.

title: RAT-26 Impl: freeze contrato API rating 360 FE/BE
description:
Objetivo: congelar contrato FE/BE del rating 360 para evitar drift y desbloquear implementación.

Alcance:
- Definir payload final de endpoints `create/edit/respond/appeal/report/list`.
- Congelar catálogo de `error.code` y mapping por estado HTTP.
- Definir política de versionado (`/api/v1`) y compatibilidad.
- Definir campos y límites de tags/mensajes/comentarios para FE.

Fuera de alcance:
- Implementación de backend.

Criterios de aceptación:
- Documento único firmado por FE + PM + BE.
- Lista cerrada de payloads y errores publicada en docs/reviews.

title: Implement control-plane guardrail: block implicit terminal reopen without resume:true
description:
Parent: [RAT-465](/RAT/issues/RAT-465)

Context:
- Repeated no-delta lifecycle mutations are reopening terminal issues (e.g. RAT-314 `done -> in_progress`) under status-change wakes without new comments/scope.
- Current repository does not own the control-plane runtime; implementation must be done in the Paperclip issue lifecycle engine.

Scope:
1. Enforce terminal-state transition guard in `/api/issues` lifecycle transitions: `done`/`cancelled` cannot reopen unless request includes `resume: true` and auditable reason/provenance.
2. Enforce checkout non-mutation safety: checkout must never implicitly reopen terminal issues.
3. Dedupe no-delta `issue_status_changed` wake paths so terminal-finalization events do not requeue/reopen work.
4. Persist transition provenance (`actor`, `source`, `reason`) for lifecycle mutations that change active/terminal state.

Acceptance:
- Negative regression: terminal issue + automation/checkout/no-resume => remains terminal.
- Positive regression: explicit human `resume:true` + reason => reopen allowed with audit fields.
- No-delta status-change replay does not produce reopen churn.
- Post test evidence links and touched runtime paths in child thread

title: Control-plane: enforce done->in_progress reopen guard with scoped-input gate
description:
Implement issue status governor guard in control-plane lifecycle runtime so done issues cannot auto-flip to in_progress without valid scoped reopen input.

Scope:
- Transition engine: block done->in_progress unless explicit resume/scoped trigger is present.
- Checkout/wake paths: non-mutating when no valid reopen signal exists.
- Regression tests: no-input done->in_progress blocked; explicit scoped resume allowed.
- Docs: document reopen trigger rules in workflow automation docs.

title: Implementar guardrail terminal: impedir reopen sin resume:true
description:
Implementar en la capa de transicion de estado el bloqueo de reapertura automatica de issues terminales (`done`/`cancelled`) cuando no exista `resume: true` explicito.

Alcance:
- Gate en transicion de estado para rechazar mutacion terminal->activa sin `resume: true`.
- Registrar `change_source` y `change_reason` minimos para auditoria de transicion.
- No cambiar semantica de otros estados fuera de este guardrail.

Criterios de aceptacion:
1. Sin `resume: true`, issue terminal no pasa a `todo`/`in_progress`.
2. Con `resume: true`, la reapertura funciona con rastro de auditoria.
3. Se agrega test de regresion equivalente a escenario RAT-35.

title: Provide compatible SQL client/runtime path for RAT-157 warehouse execution
description:

Problem:

Required:

2. Verify `psql --version` and `psql -Atqc "select now();"` run successfully (non-sensitive output only).
3. Post proof in [RAT-157](/RAT/issues/RAT-157).

2) Where infra is ready, transition to actionable state and publish execution window.
3) Escalate policy or product-decision blockers back to PM/CEO with decision request.

Done when: the cluster has no ambiguous blockers and each issue has a concrete path to completion.

title: Reliability fix: auto-resolve detached heartbeat runs stuck in running state
description:
Classification outcome from [RAT-388](/RAT/issues/RAT-388): actionable reliability incident (not a false positive).

Observed evidence:
- Run [a388ed24-1e25-4d58-afb6-4625a325036b](/RAT/agents/8dd474b9-148d-4918-9f17-34a47b499e08/runs/a388ed24-1e25-4d58-afb6-4625a325036b) remains `running` with `errorCode=process_detached`.
- Last output is still 2026-05-11T02:49:28.103Z with no finish/cancel timestamps.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Detached runs (`process_detached`) with stale output are transitioned to a terminal state (`cancelled` or `failed`) by watchdog/recovery logic.
2. Transition emits durable run + activity logs including reason and timestamp.
3. Stale-run review issue creation is deduplicated by origin fingerprint and does not create repeated unowned noise.
4. Tests cover detached-process + stale-output transition path.
5. Operator runbook documents manual fallback when automatic transition cannot execute.

Dependency:
- Blocked by [RAT-722](/RAT/issues/RAT-722) operational cancellation/re-wake for the currently stuck run.

title: Execute RAT-591 stale sweep: merge/close reopen-duplicate issue cluster onto RAT-568
description:
Implement the consolidation policy in docs/analysis/rat-591-stale-sweep-remediation-track-2026-05-11.md.

Required actions:
1) For each listed duplicate issue, either close as merged into RAT-568 or mark blocked with blockedByIssueIds=[RAT-568 issue id] when ownership metadata must persist.
2) Move any unique repro traces into canonical artifact chain.
3) Post closure ledger (issue -> action -> rationale).
4) Escalate only if any duplicate has truly unique reproducible vector not covered by RAT-568 criteria.

Acceptance:
- No duplicate remains in_progress without unique vector evidence.
- Ledger comment posted on RAT-591.

title: QA ownership sweep: reroute stalled accessibility/testing todos to QA execution
description:
Context: QA/accessibility tasks are sitting in todo under platform coordination lanes.

Done when:

title: Residual review/ops blocked-cluster routing (CTO queue cleanup)
description:
Residual CTO review/ops blocked cluster requires routing cleanup.

Scope
- CTO-owned blocked issues in review-silent-run/productivity/handoff normalization lanes.

Execution contract
- Classify each issue as ownership mismatch, technical dependency, or product decision.
- Reassign non-CTO execution work to correct profile with takeover comment.
- For dependency-gated issues, set `blockedByIssueIds` plus explicit unblock owner/action.
- Move false-blocked items to actionable status with next execution date.

Done when

title: Review PM: consistencia de policy en playbook de moderación
description: Validar consistencia de reglas de decisión, severidades y flujo de apelaciones con política de producto. Proponer cambios para minimizar fricción y mantener confianza.

title: Product Spec: arquitectura de rating 360 (cliente, prestador, servicio)
description:
Objetivo: definir especificación funcional completa del sistema de rating/reviews 360 para servicios.

Alcance:
- Modelo de calificación multi-eje: calidad técnica, puntualidad, comunicación, relación precio-calidad, resolución de incidentes.
- Diferenciar rating del prestador vs rating del servicio puntual vs rating de la organización.
- Reglas de elegibilidad de reseña: solo transacciones verificadas/completadas, ventana temporal y casos especiales de cancelación.
- Políticas de edición, respuesta pública y apelación.
- Definición de estados de review: publicada, retenida, en revisión, no recomendada, removida.

Requisitos de UX simple/mobile-first:
- Flujo de valoración en <60 segundos con 1 mano.
- Escalas visuales claras, lenguaje sencillo y mínimo esfuerzo cognitivo.
- Diseño de carga progresiva y tolerante a conexión inestable.

Protocolo de calidad/review obligatorio:
- Review UX: UX/UI Designer.
- Review técnico: Back-End Developer.
- Review de calidad: QA Specialist.
- Mínimo 2 iteraciones de refinamiento antes de done.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Complete PRD with user stories and edge cases for all actor types
- Defined review lifecycle states and moderation triggers
- M

title: Harden auto-unblock: require RAT-157 SQL path READY before reopening RAT-235
description:
Problem: RAT-235 is repeatedly auto-unblocked in RAT-41 sweeps on deps=0 despite failing runtime gate.

Current failing evidence:
- tools/guardrails/check-rat-157-warehouse-sql-runtime-path.sh
- output: RAT_157_WAREHOUSE_SQL_RUNTIME_PATH_MISSING:rat39_source_surface
- exit: 2

Required fix:

2) Require fresh same-thread proof of RAT-157 SQL path readiness:
   - marker: RAT_157_WAREHOUSE_SQL_RUNTIME_PATH_READY
   - exit code: 0
3) If proof absent/non-ready, keep RAT-235 blocked and emit reason rat39_source_surface.

Acceptance:

title: Investigate and stop repeated auto-reopen of completed review issues
description:
Root-cause and remediate repeated status drift where completed review issues (example: RAT-109) are automatically set back to in_progress without new actionable deltas.

Scope:
- Identify automation/routine/rule that mutates done->in_progress.
- Add guard so closed review issues only reopen when new actionable context exists.
- Provide verification evidence with before/after issue lifecycle traces.

Acceptance:
- RCA documented.
- Fix deployed/configured.
- RAT-109 remains done across subsequent status-change cycles.

title: RAT lifecycle: prevent repeated status-drift reopen on completed issues
description: Repeated issue_status_changed wakes are reopening RAT-44 with no new comments or blockers. Build and apply a lifecycle guardrail so completed issues are not flipped back to in_progress unless there is explicit new actionable context (new comment, approval stage transition, blocker resolution requiring assignee action). Include detection query, root-cause note, and mitigation rollout.

title: RAT-58 Follow-up: Backend security hardening implementation
description:
Implementar remediaciones bloqueantes detectadas en `docs/reviews/rat-58-security-review-iteracion-1.md`.

Checklist:
- Reemplazar generación de IDs internos (`reviewId`, `eventId`, `correlationId`) por CSPRNG (`crypto.randomUUID`/equivalente).
- Eliminar fallback server-side para `idempotencyKey` y exigir key externa validada.
- Implementar AuthN/AuthZ en endpoints sensibles (`/reviews/:id/reports`, `/reviews/:id/appeals`, y transición de moderación).
- Endurecer integridad de eventos (firma HMAC/KMS + `previousEventHash`).
- Añadir TTL/eviction para idempotencia/rate-limit o mover a storage compartido con expiración.
- Incorporar pruebas negativas: bypass de autorización, replay/idempotencia, tamper de eventos.

Criterio de cierre:
- 0 hallazgos High abiertos del review RAT-58.
- Evidencia de tests y actualización de documentación técnica.

title: MVP Backend Core: Discovery + Booking + Review APIs
description:
Implement domain entities, persistence model, and API contracts for provider discovery, booking requests, and verified reviews.

Scope:

- Enforce review eligibility from completed bookings only.
- Publish contract examples and error taxonomy for FE integration.

Deliverables:

- Backend implementation with deterministic fixtures for QA.

title: RAT-317.2 TalkBack evidence run (Android/Chrome)

title: Frontend Implementation: módulo de ratings/reviews mobile-first
description:
Objetivo: implementar UI productiva para creación, lectura y gestión de reseñas con foco mobile-first.

Alcance:
- Implementar componentes: rating composer, review card, trust badges, filtros, report modal, respuesta de prestador.

Protocolo de calidad/review obligatorio:

title: Ranking Science: score robusto (Bayesian + recencia + confiabilidad)
description:
Objetivo: diseñar el algoritmo de ranking reputacional para evitar sesgo por muestras chicas y manipulación.

Alcance:
- Definir score compuesto con: promedio bayesiano, volumen de reseñas, recencia, confiabilidad de reseña y severidad de incidentes.
- Reglas de display de score cuando N es bajo para evitar engaño estadístico.
- Simulación offline con datasets sintéticos y sensibilidad a fraude.

Referencias base:
- Enfoques bayesianos para priorizar certeza frente a muestras pequeñas.
- Penalización temporal y factores de riesgo para ranking más estable.

Protocolo de calidad/review obligatorio:
- Review CTO para arquitectura de scoring.
- Review Researcher para validez de supuestos.
- Review QA sobre reproducibilidad.
- Dos iteraciones mínimas con ajuste de parámetros.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Scoring formula and parameter rationale fully documented
- Simulation report includes edge cases and anti-gaming behavior
- Display rules for low sample size explicitly defined
- Two review rounds completed with parameter updates

title: RAT-10A Simulacion sintetica + calibracion iteracion 1

Trabajo requerido en el repo/runtime correcto:
1. Gate terminal-state: issues `done/cancelled` no pueden volver a `todo/in_progress` sin `resume: true` explícito y auditable.
2. Checkout non-mutating para issues terminales sin resume.
3. Dedupe de wakes `issue_status_changed` sin delta (sin comentario/scope/blocker/assignee).
4. Cobertura de regresión para replay de wake+checkout y no-reapertura automática.

Acceptance:
- Vector de reopen cerrado en runtime de issues.
- Evidencia ejecutable y/o test de regresión adjunta en el thread.

title: UX/UI Mobile-First: flujo de reseñas ultra simple y usable
description:
Objetivo: diseñar la experiencia móvil más simple y confiable para crear, leer y entender reseñas de servicios.

Alcance:
- Diseñar flujo post-servicio de rating en 3 pasos máximos.
- Componentes para leer reputación rápidamente: score global, distribución, etiquetas de confianza, highlights útiles.
- Modo accesible para baja alfabetización digital y usuarios mayores.
- Prototipos responsive con prioridad absoluta en pantallas pequeñas.

Decisiones UX críticas:
- Evitar prompts agresivos/repetitivos; momento óptimo para pedir feedback.
- Mostrar señales de confianza sin saturar: “reseña verificada”, “reseña útil”, “respuesta del prestador”.
- Diseñar anti-dark-patterns y microcopy neutral.

Protocolo de calidad/review obligatorio:
- Review PM para objetivos de negocio.
- Review Front-End Developer para factibilidad.
- Review Customer Success para claridad real de lenguaje.
- Dos rondas mínimas de test/iteración con hallazgos accionables.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Prototype includes create/read/respond/report review flows for mobile first
- Accessibility and readability checklist completed
- At least 2 usability test rounds with concrete iteration log
- Approved by PM + FE + Custom

Owner:

title: Stop RAT productivity-review reopen churn (no-delta guard + follow-up route)
description:
## Objective
Implement control-plane guardrails so completed productivity-review issues do not reopen without delta evidence, and restore follow-up issue creation route availability.

## Scope
- Add no-delta reopen guard for completed productivity-review issues.
- Ensure terminal issues require explicit `resume:true` to restart follow-up execution.
- Restore/create reliable follow-up issue creation path for review workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A completed productivity-review issue stays terminal on status-change wakes unless new comment/evidence delta exists.
- Reopening requires explicit `resume:true` mutation and is audit-visible.
- PM can create follow-up issue from review lane without API route failure.
- Add/attach regression test coverage for reopen + follow-up creation paths.

title: Provide arm64-compatible psql in CTO runtime
description:
Parent: RAT-151

New blocker after credential-propagation children completed: runtime cannot execute `psql` due architecture mismatch.

Evidence from CTO heartbeat (arm64 host):
- `psql -Atqc "select now(), current_user, current_database();" </dev/null` -> `zsh: bad CPU type in executable: psql`
- `which psql` => `/usr/local/bin/psql`
- `file /usr/local/bin/psql` => `Mach-O 64-bit executable x86_64`
- `arch -x86_64 /usr/local/bin/psql ...` also fails (`Bad CPU type`) indicating Rosetta path unavailable for this runtime context.

Required action:
- Install/provide arm64 PostgreSQL client (`psql`) in CTO execution runtime path, or adjust runtime image/toolchain to include compatible binary.
- Confirm `psql --version` executes successfully in CTO heartbeat shell.
- Then re-run non-interactive validation command for RAT-151.

Acceptance:
- `psql` executable runs on arm64 in CTO runtime shell and can execute test query non-interactively.

title: Propagate warehouse credentials to CTO runtime for RAT-143 SQL execution

RAT-144 is marked done, but this runtime still lacks effective warehouse auth. `psql -Atqc "select now(), current_user, current_database();"` prompts for password and fails with `fe_sendauth: no password supplied`.

Action required:
- Ensure credential propagation to this execution context (or provide a secure DATABASE_URL path consumable by this agent runtime).
- Validate with successful non-interactive `psql -Atqc "select now(), current_user, current_database();"`.

Acceptance:
- Auth works non-interactively in CTO runtime
- RAT-143 can execute RAT-39 SQL blocks 2 and 3 and publish KPI deltas

title: Inject warehouse credentials into CTO runtime env for RAT-151
description:

Action required:
- Set secure DB credentials in environment `f695d27f-e083-4f41-a6f3-0ebe6ae7deff` (or runtime overlay) so agent 73aae037-dfd9-4fbe-9f29-661086bc2b71 receives either valid PG* vars or DATABASE_URL at process start.
- Avoid plaintext in issue comments; use secret store/runtime env controls only.
- Post confirmation comment in RAT-151 when injected.

Acceptance:
- CTO runtime can execute `psql -Atqc "select now(), current_user, current_database();"` without interactive password prompt.

title: Trust & Safety: detección de fraude/manipulación de reseñas
description:

- Definir señales de riesgo: bursts anómalos, grafos de colusión, repetición de patrones lingüísticos, cuentas de riesgo.
- Política de acciones automáticas y manuales: retener, despriorizar, no recomendar, escalar a revisión humana.
- Marco de falsos positivos/negativos y estrategia de apelación.

Referencias base:
- Patrones de recomendación/no recomendación y reevaluación periódica estilo Yelp.
- Uso combinado de señales automáticas + revisión humana (como marketplaces de escala).

Protocolo de calidad/review obligatorio:
- Review Back-End Developer para integración operativa.
- Review Researcher para robustez metodológica.
- Review QA para escenarios adversariales.
- 2 iteraciones mínimas antes de cierre.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Fraud signal catalog with thresholds and actions documented
- Automated + manual moderation workflow defined and testable
- False-positive/false-negative monitoring plan defined
- Two review cycles completed with adversarial test feedback

title: Inject DB credentials directly into CTO process env (API config is redacted)
description:

- `which psql` -> `$AGENT_HOME/bin/psql`
- `psql --version` -> `18.3`
- Probe still fails locally (`/tmp/.s.PGSQL.5432` no server), indicating no remote connection vars present.
- `/api/environments/{envId}` returns `config: {}` with `configRedacted: true` and `metadataRedacted: true`; no credential keys are retrievable in this context.

Required action:
- Platform/CEO must inject effective DB credentials directly into the CTO heartbeat process environment (or adapter/runtime overlay) before command execution, rather than relying on runtime API fetch from redacted config.
- Confirm `PGHOST/PGPORT/PGDATABASE/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD` or `DATABASE_URL` are present at process start for CTO run.

Acceptance:
- In plain CTO heartbeat shell (no manual secret copy), `psql -Atqc "select now(), current_user, current_database();" </dev/null` succeeds non-interactively.

title: Rating Research: benchmark global y requisitos para Argentina
description:
Objetivo: producir el benchmark de referencia para diseñar el mejor sistema de rating/reviews de servicios local del mercado argentino.

Alcance:
- Analizar patrones de confianza y reseñas en Airbnb, Uber, Yelp, App Store y Google Play.
- Traducir hallazgos en decisiones concretas de producto para nuestro marketplace de servicios.
- Documentar riesgos regulatorios/compliance aplicables a UGC, moderación y manipulación de reseñas en Argentina.

Lineamientos de referencia externa (base mínima):
- Airbnb: ventana de 14 días y publicación diferida para evitar represalias/reviews sesgadas.
- Yelp: separación entre reseñas recomendadas y no recomendadas con reevaluación continua.
- Uber: confidencialidad parcial de razones de rating para evitar retaliación directa.
- Apple/Google: políticas anti-manipulación, prompts no intrusivos y moderación de contenido.

Entregables:
- Documento de benchmark con matriz: feature, objetivo de negocio, riesgo de abuso, costo de implementación, impacto esperado.
- 15 decisiones priorizadas para MVP + 15 para post-MVP.
- Recomendación explícita de qué NO implementar en MVP.

Protocolo de calidad/review obligatorio:
- Review técnico: CTO.
- Review de negoc

description:

title: Fix RAT-201 productivity-review auto-reopen loop
description:
Parent: [RAT-201](/RAT/issues/RAT-201)

Observed behavior:
- [RAT-201](/RAT/issues/RAT-201) was closed as `done` multiple times, but repeatedly transitioned back to `in_progress` without new comments/evidence.
- Source review target [RAT-192](/RAT/issues/RAT-192) is already `done`.

Requested investigation:
1. Identify which automation/workflow is reopening completed `issue_productivity_review` issues.
2. Add guardrail so `done` productivity-review issues are not auto-reopened when there is no new trigger evidence.
3. If reopen is intentional, require a new delta (new run/comment/status signal) and include rationale in issue comment.

Acceptance criteria:
- Root cause documented in issue comment.
- Reopen loop for this class of issue is resolved.
- [RAT-201](/RAT/issues/RAT-201) remains terminal unless genuinely new evidence appears.

title: Prevent repeated status-drift reopen loops for resolved productivity-review issues
description:
RAT-103 has repeatedly auto-reopened to in_progress via issue_status_changed with no new comments/evidence and source issue RAT-70 already done. Implement/verify lifecycle guard so terminal productivity-review issues do not reopen without fresh delta (comment/evidence/explicit resume action).

Acceptance:
- Reproduce with RAT-103-like path.
- Add/verify control-plane guard and tests for no-delta reopen suppression.
- Post verification evidence and close the loop.

title: Fix productivity-review auto-reopen churn for terminal source issues
description:
Repeated wake/reopen loop observed on completed productivity review issue [RAT-208](/RAT/issues/RAT-208).

Context (verified on May 11, 2026):
- Source issue [RAT-135](/RAT/issues/RAT-135) is `done` and unassigned.
- Review issue [RAT-208](/RAT/issues/RAT-208) has been re-opened multiple times despite explicit closure comments and no new source activity requiring review.

Required outcome:
- Identify and fix control-plane rule causing `issue_productivity_review` records to re-open after terminal closure when source issue is already terminal (`done`/`cancelled`).
- Add guardrail test coverage for non-reopen behavior.
- Backfill/normalize any currently flapping productivity review issues in this project.

Acceptance criteria:
1. RAT-208 stays `done` for 24h without auto-reopen when RAT-135 is unchanged and terminal.
2. Regression test added for reopen suppression condition.
3. Brief root-cause note posted in issue thread with changed component and verification evidence.

title: Stop RAT-180/RAT-132 status-drift reopen loop
description:
Investigate and fix repeated lifecycle drift where [RAT-180](/RAT/issues/RAT-180) reopens to in_progress and [RAT-132](/RAT/issues/RAT-132) reverts to todo without new scope/comments.

Deliverable:
- Identify root trigger (automation/sweep/policy) causing status reversions.
- Implement guardrail so closed productivity-review issues and board-blocked placeholder issues do not auto-reopen without new evidence.
- Post verification evidence with before/after state transitions.

title: Investigar y corregir reopen/status-drift automático en RAT-11
description:
Se observan múltiples wakes `issue_status_changed` que reabren `RAT-11` en `in_progress` sin comentarios ni cambios de scope tras haber quedado `done`. Objetivo: identificar origen (workflow/automation/policy), corregir regla de transición y prevenir reaperturas espurias en issues cerrados.

Criterios de cierre:
- Root cause documentada.
- Regla/automatismo ajustado para evitar reopen sin delta real.
- Validación con al menos 1 ciclo sin reopen espurio.

Referencias: [RAT-11](/RAT/issues/RAT-11).

title: RAT-51 Follow-up: corregir reapertura automatica de estado (done -> in_progress)
description:
Problema observado: RAT-51 fue reabierto automaticamente multiples veces via wake `issue_status_changed` sin cambios de alcance ni comentarios nuevos, despues de quedar `done` y sin blockers activos.

Objetivo:
- identificar trigger/regla que vuelve a `in_progress` issues cerrados
- aplicar fix para evitar loops de wake en issues completados
- validar con evidencia en historial de estados

Criterio de cierre:
- issue fuente no vuelve a reabrirse automaticamente en 24h
- causa raiz documentada y mitigacion aplicada

title: Stabilize productivity-review lifecycle to prevent auto-reopen loops
description:
Resolved productivity review issues are being reopened by telemetry-only `evidence refreshed` events without new risk deltas.

Scope:
- Define lifecycle rule so telemetry refreshes do not flip resolved reviews back to `in_progress` unless risk state materially changes.
- Preserve wake behavior for genuine regressions (new blocker delta, no movement window exceeded, or churn threshold breach).
- Document expected behavior for managers triaging productivity-review issues.

Acceptance criteria:
- A review closed as `done` remains closed across telemetry refresh cycles when risk signals are unchanged.
- Reopen occurs only on configured material-delta triggers.
- One test/demo trace attached showing non-reopen on refresh and reopen on real delta.

title: Fix workflow: RAT-54 auto-reopen without new context
description:
Objective: stop RAT-54 from being auto-moved to in_progress when there is no new comment/scope/event.

Acceptance:
- RAT-54 remains blocked/done unless a real work event occurs.
- No further heartbeat churn from status-only reopen.

title: Normalize cycle-blocked recovery dependencies (RAT-810/RAT-808/RAT-778)
description:
Follow-up from RAT-817 validation sweep.

Findings
- Attempts to normalize RAT-810 -> RAT-809, RAT-808 -> RAT-807, RAT-778 -> RAT-671 currently fail with `Blocking relations cannot contain cycles`.

Scope
- For each pair, remove reverse blocker edge first (if present), then set intended direction on recovery issue.
- Reassign each recovery issue to execution owner aligned with blocker issue assignee.
- Add concise thread comment on each corrected issue with next owner/action.

Acceptance
- No cycle errors remain for listed pairs.
- Each recovery issue has explicit blocker edge and non-CEO execution owner.

title: Investigate issue status write/read mismatch on /api/issues/:id
description:
Observed during RAT-365 closeout: PATCH /api/issues/{id} returns payload echoing status=done, but immediate GET /api/issues/{id} can still report status=in_progress with older updatedAt/completedAt values.

Impact:
- Agents can record contradictory durable state in issue threads.
- Closeout automation may loop due stale read model or write-path inconsistency.

Scope:
1. Reproduce with integration test (PATCH then GET consistency assertion).
2. Identify whether this is write rejection, stale read source, or response echo bug.
3. Fix API behavior so PATCH response and subsequent GET are strongly consistent for issue status transitions.
4. Add regression test coverage in routes/service layer.

Context:
- Seen on RAT-365 / RAT-190 coordination run on 2026-05-11.

title: Investigar loop: issue done se reabre a in_progress en wakes automáticos
description:
Contexto:
- RAT-38 está funcionalmente cerrado.
- En múltiples heartbeats, el issue vuelve a `in_progress` sin comentarios nuevos, generando retrabajo operativo.

Objetivo:
- Identificar por qué el harness/flujo de ejecución vuelve a mover el issue a `in_progress` tras quedar `done` y, en algunos intentos, tras quitar assignee.
- Proponer y aplicar fix de plataforma/proceso para evitar reaperturas automáticas en issues terminales.

Criterios de aceptación:
1. Root cause documentada con evidencia.
2. Cambio aplicado o workaround validado que evita nuevas reaperturas automáticas del mismo patrón.
3. Nota de cierre con impacto y cualquier riesgo residual.

title: Prevent repeated reopen drift for completed productivity-review issues

2. Add guardrails/idempotency so status transitions honor terminal `done` absent new trigger data.
3. Add regression test covering repeated wake cycles with no new comments/evidence.

Impact:
- Prevents review-noise churn and unnecessary CTO wake loops.

Source context: [RAT-330](/RAT/issues/RAT-330)

title: Implement terminal done-state no-delta reopen guard in control-plane lifecycle
description:
Parent: [RAT-412](/RAT/issues/RAT-412).

Objective:
Implement the control-plane lifecycle fix so completed productivity-review issues do not auto-reopen to in_progress without new evidence.

Scope:
- Issue transition engine: terminal states (done/cancelled) immutable unless explicit resume:true.
- Checkout path: cannot implicitly reopen terminal issues.
- Wake/status dedupe: no-delta status-change events cannot reopen/requeue terminal issues.

Acceptance:
1. Reproduce RAT-195/RAT-412 reopen path in lifecycle runtime fixture.
2. Add guard(s) for transition, checkout, and no-delta wake paths.
3. Add at least one direct regression test for each path.
4. Attach API/service replay evidence showing:
   - no reopen without resume:true,
   - reopen allowed with explicit resume:true + reason,
   - no-delta status-change wake does not reopen.

Links:
- [RAT-412](/RAT/issues/RAT-412)
- [RAT-195](/RAT/issues/RAT-195)
- docs/analysis/rat-412-rat-195-done-state-reopen-loop-no-new-evidence-2026-05-11.md

- Preventive fix or explicit policy update applied.
- Verification note showing RAT-89 remains stable in done without manual re-close.

title: Fix RAT-195 done-state reopen loop without new evidence
description:
Parent: [RAT-195](/RAT/issues/RAT-195)

Problem:
- RAT-195 has been closed as done multiple times, but it is auto-returning to in_progress without new human comments or new source evidence.

Expected behavior:
- Productivity review issues already marked done should not auto-reopen unless fresh evidence or human feedback arrives.

Acceptance:
1. Reproduce and identify state transition source that reopens done review issues.
2. Add guard so done issues stay terminal without new evidence triggers.
3. Document rule in monitor/lifecycle logic and backfill one regression test if available.

title: Investigate repeated status drift reopening closed silent-run review issues
description:
Parent issue RAT-110 has been auto-reopened to in_progress multiple times without new comments or evidence. Investigate the trigger path (automation/policy/workflow) and ship a fix or guardrail to prevent closed silent-run review tickets from reopening unless new run evidence is attached.

Scope:
- Trace source of status transition events for RAT-110 on 2026-05-11.
- Identify whether run monitors, policy hooks, or stale reconciliation jobs are forcing reopen.
- Propose and implement remediation.
- Post verification evidence and close loop with root cause.

title: Fix auto-reopen rule causing RAT-16 status churn
description:
Problem: RAT-16 (completed review gate) keeps reopening to in_progress on status-only events without new comments/scope delta.

Expected:
- Completed review-gate issues should not reopen unless there is a new actionable delta (new comment, scope change, approval stage change, or explicit resume request).

Scope:
- Inspect board/workflow automation that mutates status for review-gate issues.
- Add guardrails to prevent reopen when latest state is done and no new delta exists.
- Document trigger logic and owner runbook.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Reproduce current reopen behavior in logs/rules.
- Deploy rule fix that blocks no-delta reopen.
- Verify RAT-16 remains done across two subsequent heartbeat cycles unless explicit delta occurs.

title: Investigate repeated status drift reopening done review issues
description:
Repeated wakes show review issues returning from done to in_progress with no new comments/evidence.

Scope:
- Identify automation or rule path that reopens already-resolved review issues.
- Add guardrail so issue_status_changed does not retrigger reopen on unchanged evidence.
- Define idempotent closure behavior for resume deltas.

Acceptance:
- Root cause documented with offending rule/job id.
- At least one deterministic fix or temporary mitigation landed.
- Validate no further spontaneous reopen for RAT-108-style cases in next cycle.

title: Implement lifecycle suppression for repeated status-reopen events on closed productivity-review issues
description:
## Context
[RAT-77](/RAT/issues/RAT-77) is repeatedly reopened by automated `issue_status_changed` events after normalization.

## Required outcome
Implement/switch on lifecycle suppression so repeated reopen events for closed productivity-review issues are ignored (RAT-77 pattern), then confirm [RAT-77](/RAT/issues/RAT-77) can be normalized to `done` without churn.

title: Implement stale-run dedup + terminal-source guard in runtime detector
description:
Implement RAT-244 controls in the Paperclip runtime stale/silent-run alert path.

Required:
- Skip stale-run triage creation when source issue status is done/cancelled.
- Deduplicate by (sourceIssueId, runFingerprint) with configurable cooldown (default 24h).
- One-shot recovery: clear stale activeRun pointer when source issue is terminal.
- Add regression tests for terminal guard, in-window dedup suppression, out-of-window re-alert, and pointer clearing idempotency.
- Add runbook note for expected suppression behavior.

Security spec artifact:
- /Users/martinmarquez/uber-services/docs/reviews/rat-244-stale-run-alert-dedup-security-spec-2026-05-10.md

Acceptance:
- No duplicate stale-run triage issues for same source+fingerprint during cooldown.
- Terminal source issues do not emit stale-run triage issues.
- Audit events emitted for suppression and pointer recovery paths.

title: Crear plan maestro, roadmap e issues para construir el “Uber de servicios” líder en Argentina con IA, pagos flexibles, cripto y blockchain
description:
Actuá como el CEO fundador de una startup argentina cuyo objetivo es construir el marketplace de servicios locales más confiable, simple y masivo de Argentina: una especie de “Uber de servicios”, donde usuarios puedan encontrar, contratar, pagar, calificar y recomendar prestadores de servicios individuales, profesionales, comercios y organizaciones.

La misión de la compañía es:

“Hacer que contratar un servicio confiable sea tan fácil como pedir un viaje, y que cualquier prestador argentino pueda vivir mejor de su trabajo con tecnología, reputación, IA y pagos flexibles.”

Tu tarea principal NO es implementar el producto todavía. Tu tarea es diseñar la compañía, el producto, el roadmap y crear todos los issues necesarios dentro de Paperclip para que cada agente/empleado pueda ejecutar su parte con máxima claridad.

Debés comportarte como CEO, Product Strategist y coordinador general de la empresa. Tenés que pensar como una compañía completa, no como un único desarrollador.

***

title: Content: versión final outreach multicanal + variantes A/B
description:
Objetivo
- Convertir el borrador aprobado en versión final lista para operación comercial.

Entregables
1. Email frío + follow-up final con copy pulido y placeholders estandarizados.
2. WhatsApp primer contacto + 5 respuestas de objeción con versión breve y versión extendida.
3. Guion de llamada discovery/cierre final (10-15 min) con 5 objeciones y cierre.
4. Matriz claim->evidencia final en formato reutilizable para Sales.
5. Dos variantes A/B:
- Asunto de email (A/B)
- Opening de WhatsApp (A/B)

Criterios
- Claim hero exacto: "reseñas verificadas por servicio completado" en todos los canales.
- Sin promesas numéricas no validadas.
- Mención explícita de "pago protegido + soporte ante incidentes" como pilar secundario.

Dependencia de contexto
- Base aprobada en [RAT-55](/RAT/issues/RAT-55).

3) Guion llamada discovery/cierre con manejo de 5 objeciones.
4) Tabla claim -> evidencia requerida en cada canal.

Criterios de aceptación:
- Cada guion incluye apertura, prueba de confianza, manejo de objeción y CTA.
- Consistencia de claims entre canales.
- Listo para integrar en RAT-50 antes de reactivar campaña.

title: Investigate recurring auto-reopen drift on closed review issues (RAT-113 pattern)
description:
Observed repeated status drift on RAT-113: issue returns to in_progress after explicit done closure, with no new comments/evidence.

Scope:
- Identify automation/trigger path causing closed review issues to reopen.
- Confirm whether this is expected lifecycle policy or defect.
- Propose and implement guardrails to avoid duplicate wake churn for already-closed review issues.
- Document remediation and rollout plan.

Acceptance:
- Root cause identified with reproducible condition.
- Durable fix or policy update applied.
- RAT-113-style loops no longer reproduce for two consecutive checks.

title: Investigate repeated status drift on closed productivity-review issues
description:
Source symptom: [RAT-343](/RAT/issues/RAT-343) repeatedly auto-transitions from `done` back to `in_progress` without new comments or actionable delta.

Operational impact:
- Repeated false-positive wakes for completed review work
- Duplicate closure actions and queue noise

Requested action:
1. Trace automation path/rule that mutates review issue status post-close.
2. Patch guardrail so `done` review issues do not reopen without explicit actionable trigger.
3. Document owner runbook for handling similar status-drift incidents.

Acceptance:
- No automatic reopen on completed productivity review issues unless new actionable context exists.
- Root-cause + fix note posted in this thread.

title: Prevent RAT productivity review auto-reopen churn without material deltas
description:
Parent: [RAT-340](/RAT/issues/RAT-340).

Problem:
- Completed productivity review issues are repeatedly re-opened by automation from non-material "evidence refreshed" updates, generating review churn and duplicate QA closures.

Required outcome:
- Keep completed review issues closed unless source issue has a material delta (new assignee artifact/checkpoint, blocker resolution, or explicit decision request).

Acceptance checks:
- No auto-reopen when only refresh comments are emitted and no decision-relevant fields changed.
- Auto-reopen still occurs when a material delta is detected.
- Add regression coverage or deterministic verification notes in implementation issue thread.

title: Stop RAT-79 repeated status-drift auto-reopen loop
description:
Lifecycle follow-up: RAT-79 repeatedly reopens to in_progress with no new comments or scope. Investigate and remediate issue-level reopen trigger path for already-closed productivity review issues.

Scope:
- Capture exact event source that flips RAT-79 from done -> in_progress.
- Apply idempotency guard so no-delta status-change wakes do not reopen terminal review tickets.
- Validate with replay on RAT-79 and one additional closed review issue.

Success criteria:
- RAT-79 remains done across resolved-review snooze cycles when no new deltas exist.
- No regression on legitimate resume flows (explicit resume:true or new human comments).

title: Investigate repeated auto-reopen on completed productivity review issues
description:
Repeated `issue_status_changed` wakes are reopening completed review issue [RAT-209](/RAT/issues/RAT-209) to `in_progress` without new comments, evidence requests, or scope changes.

Task:
- Identify automation/rule that reverts completed review issues to `in_progress`.
- Apply fix so completed review issues remain `done` unless new actionable context exists.
- Backfill guardrails/tests for idempotent closeout behavior.

title: Fix RAT-201 productivity-review auto-reopen loop
description:
Parent: [RAT-201](/RAT/issues/RAT-201)

Observed behavior:
- [RAT-201](/RAT/issues/RAT-201) was closed as `done` multiple times, but repeatedly transitioned back to `in_progress` without new comments/evidence.
- Source review target [RAT-192](/RAT/issues/RAT-192) is already `done`.

Requested investigation:
1. Identify which automation/workflow is reopening completed `issue_productivity_review` issues.
2. Add guardrail so `done` productivity-review issues are not auto-reopened when there is no new trigger evidence.
3. If reopen is intentional, require a new delta (new run/comment/status signal) and include rationale in issue comment.

Acceptance criteria:
- Root cause documented in issue comment.
- Reopen loop for this class of issue is resolved.
- [RAT-201](/RAT/issues/RAT-201) remains terminal unless genuinely new evidence appears.

title: Fix repeated auto-reopen for completed productivity-review issues
description: Observed repeated status drift where completed productivity-review issue [RAT-342](/RAT/issues/RAT-342) is auto-transitioned back to in_progress without new comments/evidence. Expected behavior: keep terminal done state unless explicit new actionable context arrives.

title: Fix recurring status regression for closed productivity review issues
description:
Closed productivity review issues are being reset to `in_progress` without new evidence/comments, causing repeated wakes and manual re-close loops.

Scope:
- Reproduce why `issue_productivity_review` items (example: [RAT-212](/RAT/issues/RAT-212)) transition from `done` back to `in_progress`.
- Patch lifecycle logic so a closed review remains terminal unless new evidence or explicit reopen action exists.
- Add regression coverage for this path.

Acceptance:
- Closing a productivity review issue keeps it `done` across subsequent automation cycles when no new evidence is posted.
- No repeated status-changed wakes for already-closed review issues.

RAT-41 sweep repeatedly auto-unblocks RAT-157 (`deps=0`) even though runtime credential blocker persists. This causes churn and repeated re-block cycles without progress.

1. In sweep logic, avoid moving issues to in_progress when issue comments indicate unresolved runtime credential blocker owned by CTO/Data Platform.

3. Add a note in sweep comment when unblock is dependency-only and not runtime-readiness.

Acceptance:
- RAT-157 remains blocked until credentials are injected and connectivity succeeds.
- Sweep does not re-open credential-blocked tasks solely because deps=0.

title: Enforce terminal-state resume gate for issue status transitions
description:
Parent: RAT-355

Implement backend guardrail so issues in done/cancelled cannot move to todo/in_progress unless payload explicitly includes resume=true with actor/reason.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Terminal issues remain terminal on automation/checkout/status writes without resume=true.
2) Resume path captures actor + reason in audit metadata.
3) Integration test covers terminal->nonterminal rejection without resume flag.

title: QA regression harness for terminal-state drift (RAT-35 replay)
description:
Parent: RAT-355

Build and run QA regression fixture that replays RAT-35 equivalent status lifecycle over repeated wake/checkouts.

Acceptance criteria:
1) 24h simulation/replay shows no automatic transition out of done/cancelled without resume=true.
2) Board WIP counter remains unchanged after terminal closure replay events.
3) Test report artifact linked in parent issue thread with pass/fail evidence.

title: Prevent status-change wake/checkouts from reopening terminal issues
description:
Parent: RAT-355

Add automation/runtime protections:
- Deduplicate issue_status_changed wakes for terminal-finalization-only transitions (no scope/comment/blocker/assignee delta).
- Ensure checkout path is non-mutating for terminal issues unless resume=true.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Terminal-finalization events do not generate reopen-inducing wake loops.
2) Checkout on terminal issue returns non-mutating response without resume=true.
3) Regression test reproducing RAT-35 lifecycle passes with zero unintended reopen transitions.
